anti_claim: The capsule demonstrates owner-routed generated projection drift detection over declared artifacts, source authorities, clean-receipt fingerprints, and no-write check return codes. It is not semantic drift proof, not full macro registry validation, not repair authority, and not release authority.

anti_claim_contract.rule: Organ-facing public copy must stay within the accepted organ claim ceiling and must not imply release, publication, provider, private-root-equivalence, source-mutation, runtime-correctness, or whole-system-correctness authority.

authority_ceiling.claim_ceiling: Exercises an owner-routed generated projection drift gate over bounded public fixtures: per-file SHA-256 fingerprinting of source authorities and artifacts, a prior-clean-receipt source-hash skip cache, required-artifact presence, and each owner's declared no-write check command return code. It reports a drifted owner by recomputation. It does not prove that every macro owner uses true content-diff semantics, does not repair files, does not validate the full macro registry, and does not authorize public release.

validation_rules: validate required source refs or target refs remain public-safe
when a projection owner sets require_fact_authority_lineage true, reject missing or invalid authority_ref, appearance_refs, derivation_path, guard_ref, treatment, or residual_route
preserve draft/staged status until an active v2 acceptance lane supplies validator and receipt evidence
reject release, provider dispatch, source mutation, private-root equivalence, and whole-system correctness claims
require the accepted organ validator command to recompute the bounded public fixture result
require generated receipts and acceptance receipt refs to remain body-free and public-relative
require claim ceiling, evidence class, truth-accounting bucket, and authority ceiling to match core/organ_registry.json
reject release, publication, provider-dispatch, source-mutation, private-root equivalence, and whole-system correctness claims
receipt_expectations: record validator command identity, observed result status, authority ceiling, and anti-claim
record public-relative receipt refs and avoid source bodies, credentials, provider payloads, private paths, or operator transcripts
record the accepted organ claim ceiling and do not treat validator success as release or publication authority

standard_payload.ontology: Accepted Microcosm organ standard for `generated_projection_drift_runtime`: Exercises an owner-routed generated projection drift gate over bounded public fixtures: per-file SHA-256 fingerprinting of source authorities and artifacts, a prior-clean-receipt source-hash skip cache, required-artifact presence, and each owner's declared no-write check command return code. It reports a drifted owner by recomputation. It does not prove that every macro owner uses true content-diff semantics, does not repair files, does not validate the full macro registry, and does not authorize public release.
standard_payload.teleology: Expose `generated_projection_drift_runtime` as a bounded public runtime/receipt contract with validator-backed evidence, while keeping the claim ceiling and release boundary visible to cold readers and release gates.
standard_payload.anti_purpose: Using `generated_projection_drift_runtime` as proof of broader runtime correctness, release readiness, provider authority, private-root equivalence, or claims outside its accepted organ ceiling.
failure_modes: accepted organ standard file missing or not discoverable as std_microcosm_<organ_id>.json
validator command, receipt id, or authority receipt drifts from core/organ_registry.json
bounded fixture result is trusted from prose or stale receipts instead of recomputed by the validator
public receipt embeds private paths, provider payloads, credentials, raw operator text, or source bodies
evidence class or truth-accounting bucket is overclaimed beyond the accepted organ row
green validator output is treated as release, publication, provider, private-root-equivalence, source-mutation, runtime-correctness, or whole-system-correctness authority
external check-command or tool witness absence is counted as a pass instead of a blocked or locked non-verifying state

contract_projection_basis.authority_boundary: computed standard-lattice contract only; accepted organ registry and receipt anchors bind the source row but do not grant release, provider dispatch, private-root equivalence, runtime correctness, or whole-system correctness authority.

author.purpose: create a valid Generated projection drift runtime runtime instance

refine_instance.purpose: improve an existing Generated projection drift runtime without corrupting authority

refine_standard_and_propagate.purpose: change std_microcosm_generated_projection_drift_runtime and propagate through governed artifacts plus generated lattice projections
